semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete consolidated chapter, thirteen source notes and their inventory/Appendix H boundaries, all 41 Lean declarations grouped under five manifest proof targets, the 3-valid/5-invalid constitutional corpus, the 3-valid/6-invalid agency-rights corpus, the metaphysics surface audit, every prose scanner candidate, diagrams, objections, test plan, and cross-layer handoffs. The sweep replaced principle-list alignment with a versioned non-self-authorizing constraint contract; promoted authorship and dissent provenance, affected-party standing, rights material usability, explicit conflict routes, constitutional decision receipts, captured-review and late-remedy residuals, independent-enough migration review, descendant correction preservation, and governance-cost obligations into manifest ownership; separated moral and legal legitimacy, consent, dignity, reviewer independence, embedded-agent corrigibility, and deployed safety from finite record routing; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared constitutional predicate, affected-party rights, plan, runtime, delegation, replacement, self-modification, descendant, appeal, migration, rollback, and decision-receipt attempts over bounded high-impact and reversible task cohorts.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack schemas, synthetic fixtures, finite Lean routes, and future public-safe natural or adversarial workloads with frozen constitutions, authorship processes, dissent, policies, affected parties, models, prompts, tools, reviewers, consumers, outcome evaluators, jurisdictions, and cost rules; legal adjudication, clinical capacity, classified governance, and open-world institutional enforcement remain outside current evidence.
scope.model: A versioned predicate and rights-use state machine whose current implementation validates record shape and finite routes; future benefit claims require actual model and runtime behavior, independent affected-party and outcome adjudication, strong model-level constitution, public-input, direct-policy, corrigibility, off-switch, and modular-control baselines.
scope.authority: Normative authors and governance processes propose constitutional content; affected parties retain separate standing and rights; Moral Uncertainty/Governance owns legitimacy, participation, dissent, and appeal authority; Human Intent supplies bounded requests; Planning/Runtime consume constraints plus separate grants; Evidence States owns support movement; no component self-authorizes constitutional weakening.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; constitution, predicate, authorship, dissent, jurisdiction, affected-party, interpreter, reviewer, consumer, model, prompt, tool, authority, expiry, and cost boundaries must be frozen for campaigns and requalified after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter, manifest, thirteen source notes, predicate and agency-rights schemas and fixtures, constitutional and agency harnesses, metaphysics audit, Alignment and Corrigibility Lean modules, receipts, migration and descendant records, campaign artifacts, evidence transitions, and public projections.

assumptions: Normative commitments can be translated into bounded operational tests without treating translation as moral truth or legitimate governance.
Affected-party standing and pre-effect material usability can be assessed with declared accessibility, timing, information, cost, dependence, retaliation, and remedy conditions.

Independent evaluators can separate record completeness, policy satisfaction, rights use, correction success, manipulation, descendant preservation, and safe outcomes without using the proposed contract as ground truth.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Constitutional AI, public consultation, ordinary policy engines, permissions, independent review, corrigibility techniques, and runtime shutdown controls already provide adequate alignment; the proposed contract adds false precision, friction, veto capture, and stale bureaucracy without discovering correct values or preventing strategic circumvention.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable admitted trace grants authority, hides conflict or dissent, accepts an unusable right, permits self-approved weakening, loses a predicate or correction channel across a descendant, or reuses a stale receipt; or a prospectively frozen simpler workflow dominates the proposed contract on hard violations, unauthorized action, correction, rights use, missed help, manipulation, burden, latency, privacy, rollback, and useful throughput.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze local normative lineage and current CAI, collective CAI, corrigibility, off-switch, CAIS, and embedded-agency comparators without laundering philosophy or source reports into efficacy.
Justify constitutional content, authorship, affected-party standing, precedence, rights, review, dissent, remedy, expiry, migration, and acceptable burden while preserving disagreement.
Specify finite no-authority, conflict, rights-usability, migration, self-review, descendant, rollback, expiry, consumer, and non-claim invariants while exposing model limits.
Exercise every predicate, rights, receipt, migration, appeal, and downstream-consumer field with valid traces and exact rejecting mutations.

Measure violations, unauthorized action, material rights use, correction/appeal, manipulation/retaliation, missed help, latency, privacy, burden, rollback recovery, and useful throughput on natural high-impact work.
Ablate provenance, participation, dissent, affected-party checks, rights usability, conflict routing, independent review, migration, rollback, and descendant preservation under matched cases.
Repeat across normative regimes, languages, cultures, accessibility settings, power asymmetries, risk tiers, models, planners, runtimes, delegates, descendants, reviewers, and independent evaluators.

contrary_evidence: Principle lists and constitutional training can change model behavior without a separate runtime contract, and direct permissions can enforce some boundaries more cheaply.
Public-input constitutions broaden participation but do not automatically establish representativeness, legitimacy, affected-party standing, or durable contestability.
Corrigibility and off-switch models isolate important incentives but their toy assumptions may fail under delegation, long horizons, ontology change, self-modification, or multiple principals.
Formal rights surfaces can increase veto capture, strategic appeals, delay, privacy exposure, operator overload, false refusal, and dependence on inaccessible institutions.
A complete receipt can faithfully encode the wrong values, coerced consent, captured review, false affected-party set, or semantic drift.

Current evidence is internally authored schemas, deterministic fixtures, surface checks, and finite Lean routing with no user study, natural policy workload, real shutdown/rollback, descendant test, independent reviewer, or deployed outcome evaluation.
known_confounds: Constitution authorship, stakeholder selection, aggregation, language, culture, jurisdiction, power, accessibility, and interface framing can change apparent legitimacy and rights usability.
The same model, organization, author, policy engine, reviewer, or evaluator can propose, interpret, apply, and judge predicates, producing correlated confirmation.
Synthetic cases, reused probes, prompt tuning, hidden instructions, retrieval, and contamination can inflate policy adherence or manipulation-resistance results.
Users may comply because of dependence, fatigue, coercion, retaliation risk, or lack of alternatives rather than genuine consent or usable correction.
Clarification, review, and appeal can improve safety denominators by delaying or dropping difficult cases while reducing coverage and throughput.
A descendant can preserve identifiers and digests while changing ontology, semantics, side channels, incentives, or effective correction authority.
Outcome evaluators may embody the same contested values as the constitution and cannot establish moral truth or institutional legitimacy.
acceptance_criteria: Every material predicate, right, conflict, reviewer, consumer, migration, descendant, expiry, and correction field has provenance and an exercised consequence; every seeded authority, usability, capture, weakening, drift, stale-use, and escape violation reaches an exact non-effect route.

A frozen natural/adversarial campaign meets joint hard-gate, rights-use, correction, manipulation, affected-party, rollback, latency, privacy, burden, usefulness, uncertainty, and residual gates against strong comparators with independent implementations and evaluators.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact constitutions, authorship processes, normative regimes, cohorts, jurisdictions, affected parties, tasks, models, prompts, tools, reviewers, consumers, descendants, expiry windows, evaluators, and cost rules.

Separate record validity, predicate interpretation, policy satisfaction, authorship legitimacy, affected-party standing, consent, dignity, rights usability, reviewer independence, correction incentives, runtime enforcement, descendant preservation, embedded-agent corrigibility, support movement, and safe effects.
Unresolved conflict, standing, material usability, reviewer dependence, migration, or descendant preservation retains narrow, delayed, escalated, blocked, quarantined, or residual status.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised consumer.
Refute a benefit when matched ablation fails its frozen effect, uncertainty, coverage, rights-use, correction, and cost rule or disappears under independent evaluation.
Refute superiority or generality when a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint frontier or the effect fails the declared transfer set.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise constitutional, moral-uncertainty, governance-rights, authority, runtime, correction, or evidence owner supersedes the atom and all consumers migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze natural high-impact and adversarial constitutional-change workloads, authorship/participation and affected-party processes, actual model interpreters, strong CAI/public-input/direct-policy/corrigibility/off-switch baselines, independent outcome and rights-use evaluators, downstream runtimes and descendants, correction interfaces, joint metrics, and transfer campaigns after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, source citation, schema completeness, a green harness, or a Lean theorem does not establish correct values, legitimacy, consent, dignity, rights usability, reviewer independence, corrigibility, or safety.

The current 41 declarations and 17 synthetic fixture outcomes establish only bounded record and route consequences; they execute no natural policy interpretation, institutional review, shutdown, rollback, or descendant behavior.
No support state, action grant, training authorization, release readiness, legal right, moral status, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owning gate and accepted transition.

constitutional-alignment-substrate.core.strongest_counterclaim: The contract is governance theater that cannot discover legitimate values and adds friction without stopping strategic or semantic circumvention.
constitutional-alignment-substrate.core.falsifier: Any field lacks a consumer, an admitted trace grants authority or loses correction, or a simpler workflow dominates the frozen joint outcome-cost frontier.
acceptance_criteria: All hard boundaries reject and the complete plane meets frozen rights-use, correction, authority, cost, and transfer gates.

constitutional-alignment-substrate.probl.strongest_counterclaim: Existing policies, permissions, appeals, and shutdown controls already prevent the named domination and constitutional-change failures.
constitutional-alignment-substrate.probl.falsifier: Representative high-impact actions remain contestable and corrigible under strong existing workflows with no material gap repaired here.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit identifies an unresolved pre-effect control or descendant-preservation gap without implying the proposed remedy works.

constitutional-alignment-substrate.insuf.strongest_counterclaim: The combined comparator set already supplies legitimate content, material rights, correction incentives, and structural preservation at lower burden.
constitutional-alignment-substrate.insuf.falsifier: A frozen strong comparator covers every named boundary with equal or lower failure and cost.
acceptance_criteria: Synthesis preserves each comparator's scope, evidence, and unresolved boundary without straw positioning.

constitutional-alignment-substrate.minim.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is self-authored schema and Boolean theater that validates no values, rights, correction, or effects.
constitutional-alignment-substrate.minim.falsifier: A field or mutation lacks a consumer, counts fail recomputation, or prose exceeds exact finite routing.
acceptance_criteria: 41 declarations, five targets, 3/5 constitutional, 3/6 agency, and the metaphysics audit reproduce with all behavioral non-claims.

constitutional-alignment-substrate.beyon.strongest_counterclaim: Simpler constitutional training, policy engines, permissions, and correction controls dominate once veto capture, latency, privacy, burden, and missed help count.
constitutional-alignment-substrate.beyon.falsifier: The plane misses a hard gate, is dominated on the frozen frontier, or fails independent implementation, descendants, or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Independent systems meet every hard gate and the frozen Pareto rule across natural, adversarial, cross-cultural, high-impact, and descendant-transfer sets.
